instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on Everything You Need to Know About Convertible Term Plans - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: term plan, convertible term plan, life insurance plans
Meta Description: Wondering if you should opt for a convertible term plan? Here’s what you should know about such plans before making a choice.
Body Content:
While today, you might think that a term plan is the most affordable and comprehensive life insurance policy available in the market, with changes in your life goals over the next decade, you might surely miss some of the add-on benefits that are offered by other life insurance plans.
Opting for a new plan at this later stage in life can add to your already existing financial obligations. This is where a convertible term plan comes in handy. With a convertible term plan, you get to change your coverage as per your evolving insurance needs.
What is a Convertible Term Plan?
A convertible term plan is simply a regular term plan with added features. Regular term plans offer you a life cover for a pre-determined period or term. If something untoward were to happen to you during the tenure of the policy, the sum assured under it would accrue to your listed beneficiaries.
However, after purchasing a term life insurance plan, your life goals may change. For instance, you didn’t have dependents while buying your term plan. However, 5 years along the way, you decided to get married and now you wish to secure the life of your spouse and future children with a plan that gives you both a life cover and maturity benefits.
If you have a regular term plan, you will have to purchase a whole new policy to avail of these benefits. However, with a convertible term policy, you can simply change your existing plan to accommodate these new insurance needs. Thus, simply put, convertible term plans allow you to change your existing plan into an endowment or whole life insurance plan. The specific rules of this conversion process will however vary from one insurance provider to the next.
Features of a Convertible Term Plan
Here’s a list of features you get to enjoy with a convertible term plan:
Maturity Benefits- Regular term plans only entitled your dependents to a death benefit payout if you happen to pass during the ongoing policy tenure. However, if you convert your term plan into an endowment policy, you will be entitled to a maturity benefit as well.
Steady Premiums- Your premium rates will remain the same, even if you decide to change your convertible term plan into an endowment or whole life insurance plan. Since these rates are decided at the onset of buying the policy, they won’t be affected by your decision to convert the plan. This implies that you will get to enjoy add-on benefits without a significant rise in your premium payments.
No Secondary Risk Assessments- Insurance companies generally undertake a risk assessment process to determine your sum assured value and premium rates. While you will have to go through this risk assessment process when buying a convertible term plan, you won’t have to endure it for a second time when converting your plan.
Tax Benefits- Under Section 80C of the Indian Income Tax Act, you can avail of tax deductions of up to ₹ 1.15 lakhs for your convertible term plan premium payments. Moreover, the maturity and death benefits you derive from the plan will also be eligible for tax deductions under Section 10 (10D) of the same Act.
Convertible Term Insurance vs. Renewable Term Life Insurance: Which is Better?
With a convertible term policy, you have the freedom of changing it into an endowment plan or a whole life policy, at a later date. Renewable term life insurance plans essentially allow you to extend the life cover tenure that’s guaranteed under your term plan.
However, this extension does come at a hiked premium rate because your insurance provider will access your age and health when underwriting the extension. Moreover, you will not earn any maturity benefits under such plans. These drawbacks make a convertible term plan a much better and more prudent option to consider than a renewable term life policy.
Benefits of Opting for Convertible Term Plans
Payment Flexibility- Since you can pay your convertible term plan premiums on a monthly, quarterly, or yearly basis, these life covers are not too hard on your wallet. Thus, you won’t be overburdened with premium payments when you also have to meet other financial obligations.
Add-On Riders- You can always opt for add-on riders to enhance the coverage umbrella of your convertible term plan. Opting for the critical illness rider and an accidental disability rider can help ensure that your emergency expenses remain covered in the event of an unpredictable crisis.
Affordable Options- Convertible term plans are affordable insurance products.  You get to enjoy the same premium rates even if you convert these plans into, say, an endowment policy, which would usually have a higher premium rate than a regular term plan.
Prolonged Financial Stability- With a convertible term plan, you can choose from a range of payout options. For instance, if you’re the sole earning member of the family, you would want to ensure the financial stability of your family in your absence. In such a case, you can opt for the monthly payout option that would substitute your monthly income to help meet the regular expenses of your dependents.
Wrapping Up
It is important to remember that for some plans, conversion is an in-built feature, while for some others, it may be an add-on benefit that you would have to opt for. Moreover, since no automatic conversions are possible, you would have to file a request with your insurance provider for the initiation of the conversion process.
